title: Continuous Pretraining vs. SFT
concept: Continuous pretraining learns raw domain terminology; SFT teaches the model to answer queries about that terminology in a specific assistant style.

exercises.prompt: What are the two main steps used to turn a base AI model into a specialized assistant?
exercises.explain: Continuous pretraining is about learning new raw domain words, while SFT is about teaching the model how to act like a helpful assistant.

cards.title: From Generalist to Expert
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how a general AI becomes a specialized doctor or lawyer? It takes a two-step training process: learning the facts first, and then learning how to talk to humans!

cards.title: Two Steps to Specialization
cards.body: Continuous Pretraining feeds an AI raw, specialized text (like medical journals or legal codes) so it learns new vocabulary. Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) uses curated question-and-answer pairs to teach the AI how to behave like a helpful assistant using that new knowledge.

cards.title: The Medical Assistant
cards.body: Imagine you are studying to become a medical receptionist. Continuous pretraining is like reading medical dictionaries cover-to-cover to learn all the terms. SFT is like practicing mock phone calls to learn how to politely answer a patient's questions using those terms.

cards.title: Building a Law Bot
cards.body:
If you want an AI legal assistant:
1. Continuous Pretraining: Feed it 10,000 raw court documents so it learns what words like 'tort' and 'injunction' mean.
2. SFT: Train it on prompts like 'Explain this law to a client' followed by safe, helpful answers.

cards.title: Books Aren't Enough
cards.body: People often think that feeding an AI medical books (pretraining) is enough to make it a medical chatbot. But without SFT, the AI might just continue writing the textbook instead of answering your specific questions!

cards.title: The Two Phases
cards.body: Compare the two distinct phases of training a specialized AI assistant.

right.text: Learns to answer questions in a helpful chat style

left.text: Reads raw documents to learn words & facts

exercises.prompt: Why would a developer use continuous pretraining on an AI?

options.text: To learn the raw terminology of a specific industry
To teach the AI to introduce itself politely
To test if the AI can write code
To delete old, useless memories from the AI

exercises.explain: Continuous pretraining is designed to feed the model large amounts of raw text so it can absorb domain vocabulary and concepts.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Continuous pretraining alone is enough to make an AI behave like a conversational assistant.
exercises.explain: Continuous pretraining teaches the vocabulary, but SFT is required to train the model to respond in a helpful, conversational Q&A format.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of AI training.
exercises.sentence: While pretraining helps the AI learn raw facts, we use [blank] to teach the AI how to behave like a polite chatbot.
exercises.explain: SFT (Supervised Fine-Tuning) uses structured chat data to teach the AI how to format its output as a helpful response rather than just completing raw text.

exercises.prompt:
A user asks: 'What is a contract?'
AI A responds: '...a contract is a legally binding agreement between two parties that...'
AI B responds: 'A contract is a legal agreement between parties. How can I help you draft one today?'
Which model shows the effect of SFT?

options.text: AI A is using SFT because it completes the sentence.
AI B is using SFT because it answers the user's question directly.

exercises.explain: AI A behaves like a raw document completing text (typical of pretrained-only models). AI B is acting like a conversational assistant (typical of SFT).

exercises.prompt: You want to build an AI that can answer patient questions about rare skin conditions. How should you split the work between continuous pretraining and SFT?

options.text: Pretraining teaches it to speak politely, while SFT teaches it raw facts.
Pretraining makes the model faster, while SFT makes it slower but smarter.
Pretraining feeds it raw domain text, while SFT trains it on question-and-answer pairs.
They are the same process, but pretraining uses smaller text files.

exercises.explain: Continuous pretraining feeds the model raw text (like medical journals) so it learns the vocabulary. SFT uses dialogue pairs (like Doctor-Patient questions and answers) so the model learns how to act like a helpful assistant using that vocabulary.
